---
title: ""
page-layout: full
sidebar: false
title-block-banner: false
---

::: {.home-hero}
::: {.home-hero-copy}
# Wen Research Group

We study water and elemental cycles in coupled human and natural systems, combining geochemistry, field observations, laboratory measurements, and environmental data science.

Based in the [Department of Earth and Environmental Sciences](https://artsandsciences.syracuse.edu/earth-sciences-department/){target="_blank"} at [Syracuse University](https://www.syracuse.edu){target="_blank"}, our group develops tools for understanding water quality, global water and elemental cycles, subsurface fluid transport, and Earth-surface processes.

::: {.home-actions}
[Research](/research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[People](/people.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Join Us](/joinus.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Education](/education.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[Contact](/contact.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::
:::

::: {.home-hero-media}
![](assets/artworks/wen-research-group.jpg){.hero-image fig-alt="Thematic illustration of Research Projects in the WEN Research Group"}

Thematic illustration of [Research](/research.qmd) Projects in the WEN Research Group.

::: {.figure-credit}
Illustrated by [MENG GRAPHICS LLC](https://xiaomengfu.myportfolio.com){target="_blank"}. Copyright © 2024 Tao Wen. All Rights Reserved.
:::
:::
:::

## Work With Us

We welcome conversations with prospective graduate students, undergraduate researchers, postdoctoral scholars, visitors, and collaborators interested in hydrogeochemistry, noble gas geochemistry, environmental data science, and open education. Please [contact Prof. Wen](/contact.qmd) to start a conversation. Prospective students and postdoctoral scholars can also review the [Join Us](/joinus.qmd) page for current opportunities in the group.

## Syracuse, New York

Our group is located on the Syracuse University campus in Syracuse, New York.

```{r}
#| echo: false
library(leaflet)
leaflet() %>%
  addTiles() %>%  # Add default OpenStreetMap map tiles
  addMarkers(lng=-76.136393, lat=43.037359)
```
